question_text: A gas container has a pressure of 100 kPa at room temperature. The same container is heated until the temperature doubles (in Kelvin). Calculate the new pressure of the gas, assuming the volume stays constant.
explanation: For a gas at constant volume, pressure is directly proportional to absolute temperature (in Kelvin). If the temperature doubles, the pressure also doubles. So 100 kPa × 2 = 200 kPa. This follows Gay-Lussac's Law: P₁/T₁ = P₂/T₂.
